definitions.technical: The process of evaluating whether an institution can survive hypothetical adverse scenarios (such as a custodian failure, market dislocation, or sudden surge in redemption demands) by modeling the impact on its reserve portfolio and liquidity position.
definitions.lay: A process where stablecoin issuers test whether they could survive worst case scenarios, like a bank holding their reserves going bankrupt, markets freezing up, or a sudden wave of customers demanding their money back, all at once.
definitions.parenthetical: (the evaluation of institutional resilience under hypothetical adverse scenarios including custodian failure and redemption surges)
definitions.legal_significance: The comment letter recommends that the OCC require stablecoin issuers to conduct stress tests modeled on the Dodd-Frank framework and Basel III LCR methodology. Proposed scenarios include simultaneous custodian failure, repo market dislocation, and a 10% redemption surge.
